title: Pecan Street Dataport — residential energy & water data
description: One of the largest resources for high-resolution residential energy-use data, extended to water use, electric transportation and regenerative agriculture, collected from a network of volunteer participants with ancillary demographic and home-audit data. Aimed at researchers and companies developing and testing energy technologies. EDiT links only; Pecan Street Inc. hosts and licenses the data.
spatialCoverage: United States (Austin TX, New York, California testbeds, and more)

distribution.format: CSV / database access via Dataport account
distribution.note: Requires a Dataport account and acceptance of Pecan Street's licence terms. Access is tiered: a free University tier offers a limited subset for individual academic research only (no resale, no sub-licensing, no funded research), while full-dataset, university-wide, and commercial access are paid. Crucially, the free academic tier does not permit funded research -- confirm the correct tier and restrictions for your use, especially grant-funded work, before relying on this data. See the licensing and pricing page (https://www.pecanstreet.org/dataport/licenses/) for current tiers, terms and prices, and the Dataport metadata document for the data dictionary.

variables: Ultra-high-resolution circuit-level electricity use (often 1-minute), plus water use, EV charging, and ancillary demographic/home-audit fields. Full field list in the Dataport metadata document and data dictionary, maintained upstream.
